schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: How \(r\) Can Act as a Bridge / Flip / Twist for DAANS: S(t)

theory.canonical_statement: 2. **Gaussian windowed insertion (narrow interjection)** Use a time-localized Gaussian envelope to insert the bridge tone only at brief times \(t_0\), producing a localized seam: \[ g(t)=\exp\!\big(-\frac{(t-t_0)^2}{2\sigma^2}\big). \] Signal sum: \[ s(t)=A_1\sin(2\pi f_A' t+\phi_1)+A_2\sin(2\pi f_B' t+\phi_2) + A_3\, g(t)\sin(2\pi f_{\text{bridge}} t+\phi_3). \] - Choose \(\sigma\) small to make the bridge narrow in time (topological “seam”). - Choose \(A_3\) small relative to \(A_1,A_2\) so it acts as a subtle connector rather than dominating.
theory.technical_interpretation: How \(r\) Can Act as a Bridge / Flip / Twist for DAANS: S(t) is an algebraic definition, written s(t)=A_1sin(2π f_A' t+φ_1)+A_2sin(2π f_B' t+φ_2) + A_3 g(t)sin(2π f_bridge t+φ_3. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from π, A_1, f_A, A_2, f_B, A_3, defining s(t). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
